prerequisites: A Devin organization with access to the Organization API.
Your Devin organization ID (org_id), which the API requires as a path segment in https://api.devin.ai/v3/organizations/{org_id}/...
A Devin API credential (api_token) authorized for your organization.

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Devin API key supplied as the connector's api_token field. The Devin API sends it in the HTTP header as 'Authorization: Bearer <api_token>'. Per the docs, service user keys carry a cog_ prefix.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a Devin API token
setupSteps.body: In your Devin organization, create an API credential (a service user key) authorized to access your organization's resources. The Devin API authenticates this key as a Bearer token. Copy the generated value for use as the connector's api_token.

setupSteps.title: Find your organization ID
setupSteps.body: Locate your Devin organization ID, which the API requires as a path segment (https://api.devin.ai/v3/organizations/{org_id}/...). Enter it in the connector's org_id field. Both api_token and org_id are required.

setupSteps.title: Set an optional start date
setupSteps.body: If you want to limit the sync window, set the optional start_date field to the earliest date from which records should be retrieved. Leave it empty to use the connector's default behavior.

sources.title: Devin API Reference - Overview (authentication, base URL, organization scoping)
